prevalence.source: WHO Europe / European sleep epidemiology studies
prevalence.trend: stable to slightly increasing with aging and mental health comorbidity

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Insomnia disorder definition with daytime impairment; evaluation for comorbid sleep apnea and mood disorders

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; CBT-I access varies and hypnotic prescribing is regulated

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care pathways often include screening for depression/anxiety and referral for sleep studies when indicated
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: CBT-I as first-line; limited-duration hypnotics; management of comorbidities and sleep apnea
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Wait times for CBT-I/sleep clinics and heterogeneous access across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong guidelines and regulated hypnotic prescribing with increasing digital CBT-I availability
